---
title: "Guilford Course Notes"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

note_entries <- c("Title", "Author", "Subject", "Pages", "University", "Abstract")

note_data <- c("Real Analysis", "Parker, Elwood G.", "foundations/analysis", "28", "Guilford College", "Analysis can be considered the study of functions between sets, where the sets have topological and algebraic structures. In this guide, the primary subject is Real Analysis, so the sets used are subsets of the real numbers; generalizations of this context are included. Three kinds of structure are assumed for the real numbers, giving the topology and algebra: metric structure, order structure, and algebraic field structure.")


tibble(note_entries, note_data) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  tab_style(
    style= cell_text(v_align = "top"),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) |>
  tab_options( table.width = "80%", column_labels.hidden = TRUE)  


```
- [Download File](./real-analysis_parkereg.pdf)

This course guide was originally published by the Guilford College Journal of Undergraduate Mathematics as a volume in the Monographs on Undergraduate Mathematics series.

This course guide may be reproduced and distributed if the following conditions are met:

- State that it is the property of the Math Department of Guilford College, and is protected under copyright by Guilford.
- Take no profit from its distribution. (You may recover copying costs if you are using these for a class.)
